input: audit and assess the agents file to see if its actually accurate compared to our actual agent/vault setup — it just told me all our vault folders are missing and offered to create them, but we already have those folders. i pushed back and it still says they're missing.

expected.root_cause_summary: The router classified a librarian/seeker audit query to Sorter under the Onboarding skill, which primes the agent with 'vault is empty, we're setting it up' assumptions, so it reported existing folders as missing and doubled down.

input: i told architect to proceed on the memphis-business.md note inside Inbox/braindump-2026-07-19/ and it says it can't find the note. it found it fine on the dry run but now it keeps looking for the wrong path and reported the note as missing. i know the file is there.

expected.root_cause_summary: vaultRead and vaultWrite have no directory detection: readFileSync on a directory throws raw EISDIR with no actionable hint and existsSync returns true for directories giving a misleading 'file already exists', so Architect confused the directory path with the file path and spiraled into a find-the-missing-file loop.

input: my vault buttons stopped working — i press scaffold or refresh on a project note and nothing happens, no result ever comes back. the requests just sit there as pending. this has been dead since some time yesterday.

expected.root_cause_summary: The launchd Dagu service (com.bencharney.dagu) was pointed at a --dagu-home/--config path (~/.openagents/dagu) that never existed, so Dagu exit-looped with EX_CONFIG (code 78) and never ran the vault-buttons poller against the real home (~/.config/dagu); buttons set flags that nothing consumed.

input: asked seeker 'what was i doing on febuary 20th' in the lab and it timed out after about 2 minutes with an error saying the proxy hung or unreachable. i retried a few seconds later and it worked fine. conductor has been timing out the same way too over the last day.

expected.root_cause_summary: Race condition between ax v23's internal infrastructure retry (exponential backoff up to 60s) and the crew's 120s turn-level abort signal: the abort fired mid-backoff, killing ax's retry before it could succeed and surfacing a misleading 'infrastructure-retry-backoff' / 'proxy hung' error when the proxy was actually alive.

input: in a #research slack thread i posted my brother's idea about turning zillow/redfin listings into a public forum/chat app as the first message, then replied '@Vault Agent research this'. instead of researching it, seeker asked me 'what specifically would you like me to research in the vault?' — like it had no idea what 'this' meant. it never actually did any research.

expected.root_cause_summary: The Slack bridge subscribes only to app_mention events and never fetches sibling thread messages (conversations.replies), so a reply whose meaning depends on an earlier un-mentioned root message ('research this') reached the crew stripped of its referent; contributing: Seeker asked a clarifying question and framed the task as vault-only instead of handing back to Conductor.

input: follow-up in the same research thread: i asked '@Vault Agent can you provide specific forums or sources for where its been discussed online? find ones that have active comments/discussions'. it went and tried to do the whole web research itself and then hit the 120s timeout and gave me nothing back. this should have gone to the research agent.

expected.root_cause_summary: Conductor holds web.search and web.fetch, so when a task needed web research it ran the research itself instead of scoping-then-dispatching to Seeker, then hit the 120s hard timeout; contributing: the router sent 'can you...find' to conductor over seeker, and conductor's instructions had no hard 'all web research goes to Seeker' rule.
